examples: There was nothing portentous or solemn about him. He was bubbling with humour.
In social politics, too, the city's contribution to 20th century thought and culture was no less portentous.
There are flaws, of course (and not just the portentous title).
As a consequence, Congress and the American public should be scrutinizing these portentous developments.
In his ponderous style, he made portentous statements about the future of information consumption.
